title: Custom App Development
excerpt:
Hello, 
We are looking to hire someone to develop a custom app for us that integrates with Monday. We have a unique quote process for custom work that we do,and we need a way to integrate this into Monday. We are looking…

posts.author: WoodDesign (Gene Miller)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello,

We are looking to hire someone to develop a custom app for us that integrates with Monday. We have a unique quote process for custom work that we do,and we need a way to integrate this into Monday. We are looking for a good English speaking partner to work with (We are in USA), then see if our process can be built into an app, then proceed from there.

Thank you.

posts.author: deepvyas (Deep Vyas)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Gene ,
I am interested to discuss first and understand your requirement.
Further, do you have this unique quote feature functional anywhere to refer in detail.

Looking forward to connect regarding the same

Good Day
Deep

Hi Gene,

I’m happy to help to building the quote process as perfectly as possible, while integrating it with Monday.

My personal expertise is in setting up and integrating Monday with apps, but I work very closely with the team at Crebos (https://crebos.online/en) who build custom applications.

Our core team is in The Netherlands and are happy to schedule a call to learn more about your requests.

Feel free to schedule it here: Calendly - Patrick Kool

posts.author: DamcoSolutions ( Neelam Bhatt)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @WoodDesign

Welcome to the community! Apps are the key to boosting the functionality of your active work processes and tools. Fortunately, monday.com allows you to build apps without knowing any programming language or using little code inputs. In your case, you can build a custom app and integrate it with your quoting process. This enables you to generate new visualizations and expand the process productivity.

If you want to build fully functional apps, hire developers from a recognized monday.com consulting company. They expand the potential of the monday.com work OS by deploying apps tailored to meet your business’s workflow and needs.

posts.cookedHtml: We can help develop a custom app integrating your unique quote process with Monday. To ensure seamless development and communication, it’s best to hire developers in the USA who understand your needs and can deliver a tailored solution. Let’s discuss further.

posts.author: avasiliev (Anton)
posts.cookedHtml: If you’re seeking a reliable partner for this, custom app development services can definitely help tailor an integration with Monday to fit your unique quote process smoothly. Communication and understanding your workflow will be key to a successful collaboration.

posts.author: ramnarayanank (Ram Narayanan)
posts.cookedHtml:
To integrate a custom quoting workflow with Monday, you will need an app that leverages Monday’s GraphQL API and webhooks for real time data sync. A product development company with expertise in API driven architectures can design middleware to handle your custom logic while ensuring scalability.

Key considerations include authentication mapping your quote process to Monday’s board structures and building a UI extension if needed. Teams like CONTUS Tech typically use agile sprints starting with requirement validation and PoC before moving into full scale app development to ensure the solution fits both workflow and long term maintainability.

posts.author: jameswood32 (James Wood )
posts.cookedHtml: Hi there! Your project sounds like a great fit for a custom Monday. com app. Monday’s API and app framework can definitely support a unique quoting process. I recommend connecting with a certified Monday partner or developer experienced in custom integrations. Start by mapping your current process, then consult with a developer to explore what’s possible. Feel free to post in the Apps & Developers section to find the right person. Good luck!

posts.author: agarwalvikas4321 (Vikas Agrawal)
posts.cookedHtml:
If you’re looking for someone to build a custom app around your Monday setup, you can consider JPLoft. We’re a mobile app development company in the USA with a strong track record of building custom software around real business workflows, not generic templates.

Our process starts by deeply understanding your custom quote logic, including pricing rules, approvals, and edge cases, and then designing an app that fits naturally into Monday. The focus is on clean integration with your boards, automations, and reporting, so the system supports how your team already works instead of forcing change.

If you’re looking for a reliable long-term partner who values clear communication and thoughtful execution, this could be a good fit.

posts.author: jhonnytom (Jhonny Tom)
posts.cookedHtml: If you’re still exploring options, the key is finding a team that understands both custom app development and how to align technology with real business workflows. Apptechies, a reliable Mobile app development company, specializes in building bespoke applications and integrations tailored to specific processes, whether it’s quoting systems, workflow automation, or third-party platform integrations. Their experience across iOS, Android, and web ensures scalable, secure solutions while keeping communication and long-term support a priority—especially important for evolving business needs.

posts.author: RemoteState (RemoteState)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi there,
I’d be glad to share some insights on setting up an efficient and well-structured app development process tailored to your requirements.
From a technical perspective, it’s important to first align on the core objectives-whether the focus is on cross-platform efficiency using technologies like Flutter, or performance-driven solutions through native Android and iOS development. Each approach offers its own advantages depending on the complexity, scalability, and user experience goals of the project.
At RemoteState, the focus is typically on understanding the broader system architecture and how different components-mobile apps, backend services, and integrations-work together seamlessly. This ensures that the final solution is not only functional but also scalable and maintainable in the long run.
If you’re exploring how to structure your app development workflow or decide between different technologies, I’d be happy to exchange thoughts and discuss what might work best for your use case.
Looking forward to hearing more about your requirements.
Best regards,
RemoteState Team
